title: Guard handleDeploy against double-submission
description: In useAutomationSetup.handleDeploy (src/features/agents/sub_connectors/libs/useAutomationSetup.ts:160), there is no in-flight guard. A user double-clicking Deploy triggers two concurrent deployAutomation calls, each potentially creating duplicate automations on the target platform (n8n/Zapier/GitHub Actions). localPhase is set to deploying but that state update is async and cannot prevent the second call started synchronously. Add a useRef-based inflight lock and disable the button while localPhase is deploying.
reasoning: Duplicate automations on external platforms are disruptive: they cost money, double-invoke webhooks, and are hard to detect after the fact. Classic non-idempotent action without a submission guard.

title: Cancel stale githubListRepos/zapierListZaps effects
description: The useEffects at useAutomationSetup.ts:120 and :131 fetch external platform data when platformCredentialId changes but have no AbortController or stale-response guard. If the user switches credentials rapidly, a slow response for the previous credential can overwrite repos/perms/zaps state for the current one, showing the wrong repo list. Add a cancellation token and drop results if the dependency has changed.
reasoning: Classic async-effect race that silently shows wrong data. Users could deploy an automation to the wrong GitHub org/repo because the UI displayed stale options. Hard to reproduce but high blast radius.

title: Stop design.result effect from clobbering user edits
description: useAutomationSetup.ts:145 unconditionally overwrites name/platform/inputSchema/timeout/fallback whenever design.result changes. If a user runs design, edits the name or schema, then triggers design again, edits are silently overwritten. Track whether the user has dirtied each field and merge rather than overwrite, or confirm before clobbering.
reasoning: Silent loss of user edits is a trust-destroying bug. Users will reasonably believe their changes were saved and deploy the wrong configuration. Happens every iteration on AI-generated designs.

title: Enforce persona-tool assignment in invoke_tool_direct
description: src-tauri/src/commands/tools/tools.rs:138 invoke_tool_direct fetches the tool definition and persona independently but never verifies the tool is actually assigned to the persona. A caller (even privileged) can invoke any registered tool against any persona, bypassing the assignment model the UI enforces. Add a repo::is_tool_assigned check and return AppError::Forbidden if unassigned.
reasoning: Authorization gap: the assignment model defines the persona capability surface, but the direct-invoke path ignores it. In plugin/multi-user scenarios this is a privilege escalation vector; even single-user it lets a buggy caller run a tool the persona was never configured for.

title: Replace substring matching in deriveStageIndex
description: useAutomationSetup.ts:36 deriveStageIndex infers automation-design progress by lowercase substring matching on backend log lines (design complete, analyzing, connected). Any wording change or localization silently regresses progress to stage 0, leaving users staring at a stuck bar. Replace with a typed stage-event protocol: backend emits a stage enum the frontend switches on.
reasoning: UI appears broken when backend wording shifts with no compile-time signal. The contract should be a typed enum, not an English substring search.

title: Cancel pending IPC on health-check reset
description: useHealthCheck.ts:183 uses genRef to abort stale writes, but testDesignFeasibility and get_persona_config_warnings are not actually cancelled. They keep running and burn LLM/IPC cost. The gen check only protects setState, not the cost already spent. Pass an AbortController into invokeWithTimeout and cancel it on reset() or when genRef changes.
reasoning: Health check makes expensive LLM/IPC calls; failing to cancel wastes budget and risks cross-persona contamination when a second check is started before the first finishes.

title: Unstable health-issue IDs break markIssueResolved
description: useHealthCheck.ts:60 generates issue IDs using Date.now()+issueSeq++ at parse time. Every fresh health check regenerates IDs, so a client holding an older issueId sees no match and markIssueResolved becomes a silent no-op. Derive IDs deterministically from issue content (hash of description+severity) so re-running health check preserves identity for unchanged issues.
reasoning: Users clicking resolved or applying a proposal after re-checking health would see no effect and no error. Pure silent failure. Deterministic IDs also enable diffing across runs.

title: credentialLinked reports true even without persona link
description: useToolImpactData.ts:195 marks a tool credentialLinked=true whenever any credential of the required service_type exists in the vault, regardless of whether the persona actually references it. A user with two GitHub credentials, none bound to this persona, still sees all GitHub tools as linked. Join against the persona tool-credential binding (credentialLinks in design_context or persona_tool config) instead of a global type set.
reasoning: Misleading UI telling users credentials are wired up when they are not, causing silent execution failures at runtime. The indicator should answer: will this tool authenticate when run?

title: Swallowed errors in tool toggle leave UI out of sync
description: useToolSelectorState.ts:124, :133, :145, :157 all use empty catch blocks (error state is set by the store slice). If the slice fails to set error state, or the failure is transient, the user sees no explanation. Optimistic assignedToolIds may be momentarily out of sync with persona.tools. Route errors through toastCatch from lib/silentCatch and ensure the store always invalidates optimistic state on failure.
reasoning: Silent failures destroy user trust. They think they added a tool, the checkbox briefly appears checked then disappears with no feedback. Routing through toastCatch ensures Sentry capture and actionable UI.

title: Undo toast may assign to wrong persona after switch
description: useToolSelectorState.ts:129 handleUndo closes over personaId. Between the setTimeout at line 120 and the clear-on-persona-switch effect firing, a fast persona switch plus undo click race could call assignTool with the wrong persona. Capture personaId inside handleToggleTool and pass to both the timer and undo action explicitly rather than depending on outer closure state.
reasoning: Cross-persona tool assignment corruption is a data-integrity bug: an undo click silently assigns a tool to whichever persona is currently selected, not the one the removal came from.

title: parseFeasibilityToHealthResult ignores malformed issues
description: useHealthCheck.ts:147 maps raw.issues assuming each entry is a string. If the backend ever returns a structured issue (object) or null/undefined entries due to a transport glitch, JSX renders [object Object] or crashes inside inferIssueSeverity. Add a shape guard at the IPC boundary: filter out non-strings, or accept both shapes explicitly.
reasoning: The contract with testDesignFeasibility is implicit; any backend evolution toward richer issues silently breaks the health panel. Defensive shape validation at the trust boundary is the right fix.

title: Clamp timeoutSecs before deploy to prevent overflow
description: useAutomationSetup.ts:58 defaults timeoutSecs=30 with no clamp or validation before deploy. A user typing 999999999 is sent verbatim to the backend, potentially overflowing timeout_ms after *1000 or pinning a deployment open. Clamp to 1..=3600 at submission and mark invalid values in the UI before deploy is enabled.
reasoning: Unbounded timeouts can pin backend resources and external platform connections. Bounds-checking at the trust boundary is textbook defensive programming with no practical user cost.
